name: Banana Cream Pie
recipeIngredient: Baked 9-inch pie shell - see Basic Crust
2 tablespoons butter, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the pie shell according to the recipe directions. Bake and let cool on a rack before filling.
In a heavy-bottomed saucepan, combine the sugar, flour, cornstarch, and salt. Gradually add the milk while stirring constantly with a wire whisk to remove any lumps. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and comes to a boil. Continue stirring for 1 minute after boiling. Remove from the heat.
Gradually stir a few teaspoons of the pudding mixture into the beaten egg yolks, mixing constantly until blended. When about half of the pudding has been added, pour the yolk mixture back into the pan, stirring until combined. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes until thick and smooth. Remove from the heat.
Gradually stir in the butter. Stir in the rum and vanilla extract. Pour the custard into a bowl and cover with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming. Let cool completely.
Whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form. Gently fold half of the whipped cream into the custard filling. Spoon a thin layer of custard over the bottom of the baked pie shell. Arrange a sliced banana on top of the custard. Alternate layers of custard and bananas, finishing with custard. Pipe or spread the remaining whipped cream evenly over the top.
Refrigerate for 3 to 4 hours, or until firm.
